Las principales medidas de conservación de esta especie son:
-Planificación y seguimiento: Plan de gestión. En Navarra se realiza el seguimiento anual de varias localidades de la población para conocer su evolución.
-Legislación, vigilancia y concienciación: Catálogos de protección legales.
-Protección y gestión del hábitat: restauración/mejora de los hábitats forestales.
-Gestión de la especie objetivo: Conservación ex situ mediante recolección de propágulos y propagación artificial.
Se considera que no son necesarias medidas, dada la dinámica poblacional.

Se han identificado varias medidas para la mejora de las poblaciones piscícolas de Andalucía, bajo el proyecto 'Programa de Actuación de Peces Invertebrados de Medios Acuáticos Epicontinentales - Años 2015-2019' (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/Programas%20de%20actuacion/programa_actuacion_epicontinentales_%20anexo_X.pdf).
Varios planes de seguimiento se han realizado en los últimos años, dando resultados negativos en los muestreos para la bogardilla: año 2014 (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/peces_invertebrados/ficha_plan_epicontinentales_2014.pdf), año 2015 (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/peces_invertebrados/ficha_plan_epicontinentales_2015.pdf) y año 2016 (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/peces_invertebrados/FichaPlanEpicontinentales2016.pdf).

Han sido tomadas varias medidas para aumentar la calidad general del hábitat, por medio de la mejora de la calidad de las aguas de los torrentes, el incremento del número de efectivos en poblaciones localizadas, control de las especies invasoras y las enfermedades fúngicas introducidas por el hombre, la creación de nuevos puntos de reproducción, la disminución de la contaminación, etc.
Recientemente en 2016, El Gobierno Balear reintrodujo la especie exitosamente, estableciendo una nueva población en el LIC Muntayes d'Artá [ES0000227], bajo el Segundo Plan de Recuperación del sapo partero balear (http://www.caib.es/sites/proteccioespecies/es/fauna/).

La especie está incluida en el 'Programa de Recuperación de Flora de las altas cumbres de Andalucía', bajo el cual se han llevado a cabo acciones de seguimiento de las poblaciones de la especie, actuaciones de conservación del hábitat mediante vallado y otras actuaciones de restauración. Como medidas de conservación ex situ esta especie se encuentra conservada en el Banco de Germoplasma Vegetal Andaluz (BGVA), de la Red Andaluza de Jardines Botánicos y Micológico, formando parte de la colección del Jardín Botánico Hoya de Pedraza, en Granada (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/altas_cumbres/Coronopues%20navasii_2015.pdf).

Las principales medidas de conservación identificadas para mantener la actual amplitud geográfica, población, y/o hábitat para la especie tanto dentro como fuera de la Red Natura 2000 son:
-Legislación, vigilancia y concienciación: Catálogos de protección legales e inclusión en la Red NATURA 2000 (9 de 27 poblaciones).
El proyecto LIFE+ RESECOM “Red de seguimiento para especies de flora y hábitats de interés comunitario en Aragón” [LIFE12 NAT/ES/000180], llevado a cabo durante el periodo 2013-2018, ha establecido una red de seguimiento de las plantas y hábitats de la Comunidad Autónoma de Aragón, con el objetivo de recopilar y generar información útil para el público y las administraciones, que permita obtener conocimiento a largo plazo sobre la dinámica de las poblaciones, especies y hábitats. Esta especie ha figurado en el listado de especies objetivo del proyecto (http://www.liferesecom.ipe.csic.es/index2.php?lang=es).

El desarrollo del 'Plan PIMA Adapta', por el Ministerio de Medio Ambiente de España desde 2015, ha venido desarrollando trabajos de restauración en sitios RedNatura 2000, con la intención de crear y restaurar nuevos hábitats para anfibios tales como charcas y fuentes: ZEC Monfragüe [ES4320077] y ZEC Montes de Toledo [ES4250005] (https://www.miteco.gob.es/es/cambio-climatico/planes-y-estrategias/PIMA-Adapta.aspx).
Es necesario reducir la carga de deshechos químicos y fertilizantes agrarios en charcas y cursos de agua, para evitar episodios de extinción. La fragmentación del hábitat es la causa de la pérdida de las poblaciones y de los individuos reproductivos, todo ello generado por las actividades agrarias intensivas.
Las principales medidas de conservación de esta especie son de tipo legal, administrativo y contractual tanto dentro como fuera de la Red Natura 2000.

El proyecto LIFE LimnoPirineus - Restauración de hábitats leníticos y especies acuáticas de interés comunitario de alta montaña en los Pirineos [LIFE13 NAT/ES/001210] restaurará el funcionamiento ecológico de 19 lagos de alta montaña a través de la erradicación – o la reducción significativa - de especies de peces exóticas invasoras. Establecerá nuevas poblaciones de especies de anfibios objetivo - Rana temporaria, Alytes obstetricans y Euproctus asper - en los lagos en los que los peces alóctonos hayan sido retirados. Las áreas seleccionadas por el proyecto se encuentran dentro de espacios Red Natura 2000 como LIC Aigüestortes [ES0000022], LIC Alt Pallars [ES5130003] y LIC Estanho de Vielha [ES513006].

Actualmente se está llevando a cabo el proyecto LIFE IREKIBAI - Ri?os abiertos: Mejorando la conectividad y los ha?bitats en los ri?os compartidos por Navarra y Gipuzkoa [LIFE14 NAT/ES/000186], que ha realizado acciones que benefician directamente a las especies como la Burtaina (https://www.irekibai.eu/burtaina/). Una de las acciones incluidas dentro de este proyecto y que se han llevado a cabo es la designación de la especie como elemento clave para las ZEC Río Bidasoa [ES2200014], ZEC Señorío de Bértiz [ES22000017] y ZEC Río Baztán y Regata Artesiaga [ES2200023].

El proyecto LIFE Biodiversidad y Trasmochos [LIFE08 NAT/E/000075] se desarrolló en País Vasco con el objetivo de mejorar y estudiar el hábitat forestal para especies de escarabajos saproxílicos entre los que se encuentra Rosalia alpina, entre 2010 y 2014 (http://www.lifetrasmochos.net/images/Trasmochos/Documentos/layman_cast.pdf).

En Galicia se está realizando el proyecto Migra-Minho, llevando a cabo acciones dirigidas a mejorar la calidad del hábitat y la conectividad del río Miño para especies migradoras anádromas como la saboga (http://migraminho.org/). Estas medidas han sido realizadas en zonas protegidas como ZEC Baixo Miño [ES1140007] y ZEC Río Tea [ES1140006].
En Navarra, actualmente se está llevando a cabo el proyecto LIFE IREKIBAI - Ri?os abiertos: Mejorando la conectividad y los ha?bitats en los ri?os compartidos por Navarra y Gipuzkoa [LIFE14 NAT/ES/000186], que ha realizado acciones que benefician directamente a las especies migradoras como el sábalo (https://www.irekibai.eu/sabalo/).

The Marsh Fritillary requires a network of sites that are close together to support its long-term survival in an area. The management of these networks requires a landscape approach as sites require light, extensive management. Management is most effective by grazing as it creates the correct sward structure that the species needs. Mowing, for example, creates a uniform structure in the vegetation such that colonies do not persist. Management of individual sites is best achieved through some sort of results-based payment system based on simple, easily measurable parameters.

The effective management of all the sites under the Natura 2000 management plans has ensured the maintenance of the conservation status of the species in the period under review. The quality of the habitat of Aphanius fasciatus is good in the larger two sites where the species is found (l-G?adira and is-Simar), but it is not good in the other two smaller sites (is-Salini and il-Mag?luq ta’ Marsaskala). 

Measures taken at il-Mag?luq ta’ Marsaskala, where the condition of the coastal lagoon is reported as not good, are actually showing an improvement in the habitat. These include the control of public access through the replacement of the fence surrounding the site, the installation of two new gates, the installation of CCTV cameras, and a patrolling schedule to reduce illegalities. Signs prohibiting dumping and littering were also installed on site. Furthermore, a rat control programme was initiated in 2018 to decrease the rat population, attracted to the accumulated dumping and litter on site. Efforts were also made to decrease the population of feral ducks and invasive species such as Arundo donax on site, to make way for other species such as Juncus maritimus, which provide the right ambient conditions for the reproduction and feeding of A. fasciatus. On the other hand, although no improvement was observed yet at is-Salini, an agreement was reached between the Government of Malta and BirdLife Malta to manage the site. In view of this, improvement in the habitat at this location is expected during the next reporting cycle.

In 2015, Nature Trust Malta and the Malta Aquaculture Research Centre within the Department of Fisheries and Aquaculture, supported by the Bank of Valletta, the Ministry for Sustainable Development, the Environment and Climate Change (MSDEC) and the Malta Environment and Planning Authority (MEPA) launched the Killifish Conservation Project (Times of Malta, 2015). The primary objectives of this project are to raise awareness, and educate the public about the importance of preserving the species and its habitat. The project also includes a captive-breeding program, securing a viable population of the species in captivity. Between 2015 and 2016, a small number of specimens were taken from il-Mag?luq ta’ Marsaskala, is-Salini and l-G?adira populations, with those taken from the former two sites being bred mainly for gene pool preservation and possibly research, with the extra stock kept for future release into the wild. On the other hand, the specimens taken from l-G?adira, which is a mixed population from two different gene pools, are being bred at the Malta Aquaculture Research Centre in biotope aquaria for educational purposes.

In 2018, the species was also declared as a National Species by the Environment and Resources Authority (ERA) to compliment current flagship species for Malta’s biodiversity. The new Species Protection Regulations (S.L. 549.120) under which A. fasciatus was declared as a National Species strengthen its level of legal protection by increasing the penalties for contraventions against such National Species, hence are expected to assist in the improvement of its conservation status in the longer term.

In addition, more broad-scale habitat management (e.g. A03 that highlights for example grazing with financial subsidies from EU) are also listed.

Single measures performed at a few sites: to combat denser forests, the county administration has initiated thinning operations and plan to reinstate grazing at some of the sites. Measures have been performed inside Natura 2000-areas only, but their extent is still limited, affecting well below half of the sites.

Conservation measures are taken that maintain populations and restore habitats. However, both the extent and the rate of measures need to be intensified to improve conservation status at the biogeographical scale. 
The list of conservation measures was based on information given by county administrative boards (that coordinate and perform most of the conservation measures on the regional scale). In addition, more broad-scale habitat management (e.g. A03 that highlights for example grazing with financial subsidies from EU) are also listed. Measures have been taken in the O, S, T, and U counties.
No measures have been reported by the Couty Administrative Boards. However, it is clear that measures are needed to improve the status of the species.

There is no species action plan for this butterfly in Sweden.  However, several suitable conservation measures have been suggested in different guidances. Abandonment of traditional grassland management close to woodland is the major threat. No species specific measures have started in continental region where the species now is extinct. No plans for reintroduction during the next 12 years.

Theese masures are ongoing efforts to manage hunting of the species,  fish populations, to limit bycatch and to reduce polution. They have already had strong positive effects on the population. In the future however, we may need to mitigate the lost of sea ice for the species, at the moment it is hard to tell when, and to what extent that will be necessary.

measures_info:
As the pressures impacting on habitat 3130 have been documented, the conservation measures required to maintain or restore it are broadly understood. These include:
1.   reversal of drainage impacts/ restoration of hydrological regime; and
2.  reducing pollution (with dissolved and/or particulate nutrients, humic substances, organic matter and fine sediment/turbidity) from agricultural, forestry, turf-cutting, golf-course and domestic and urban waste-water sources.
Significant progress has been made with domestic waste-water treatment systems through the inspection regime (under the Domestic Waste-Water Treatment Regulations) and associated grant scheme. There is also on-going substantial investment by Irish Water in urban waste water treatment and collection systems. Option (a) “Measures identified, but none yet taken” was chosen, however, as the majority of the most important measures identified, particularly in relation to agriculture and forestry, have not yet been implemented. No targeted conservation measures are currently being undertaken to restore or enhance areas of 3130 habitat within SACs. As option (a) was used, fields 8.2 to 8.5 were not filled.
The Programmes of Measures under the WFD River Basin Management Plan will help improve water quality generally; however the WFD’s default option of “Good ecological status” may not be sufficient to return the Mixed Najas flexilis lake habitat (3130) to Favourable conservation status. While further work is needed to identify effective, practical and iterative solutions and to establish interim targets, this habitat is considered to require oligotrophic or WFD high status in Ireland (NPWS, 2013a; O Connor, 2013, 2015). It also requires very clear water with low water colour and low dissolved and particulate organic matter (O Connor, 2015; Roden et al., 2018, in prep.).
Given the resources invested in the Local Authority Water Programme and the Agricultural Sustainability Support and Advisory Programme, particularly the catchment managers and scientists and agricultural advisors, the WFD should assist in maintaining and improving the conservation condition of Annex I freshwater habitats and Annex II species in SACs. To ensure the correct measures are implemented, consideration must be given to the site-specific conservation objectives and ecological requirements of the habitat/species, as well as specific information on conservation condition and impacting pressures.
Changes in the state of aquatic ecosystems, such as nutrient enrichment or hydrological changes, have multiple drivers and sources, especially when, as for Article 17, summarised at regional or national scale. Therefore, a large number of the new standard list of conservation measures are applicable and some measures may have more than one function (e.g. changes to grazing regime to prevent pollution, restore hydrological function, enhance biodiversity, etc.).
Based on the assessment of the pressures impacting on the Mixed Najas flexilis lake habitat, measures are required to address drainage impacts (CA15, CC07, CB14), pollution from agriculture (CA09, CA10, CA11, CA15, CA12, CA01, CA03, CA05, CA06, CA08), peat extraction (CC08, CC09, CC01, CC02), forestry (CB10, CB14, CB09, CB11, CB04, CB01), on-site systems (unsewered) (CF05) and urban waste-water (CF04). Measures may also be necessary to address abstraction pressures (CF11), and other hydromorphological pressures from a variety of sources, including damage to wetland hydrology (examples of appropriate measures include CA08, CE06, CF10, CF02, CF03, CJ03, etc.).

featurename: Calaminarian grasslands of the Violetalia calaminariae
measures_info:
There are no current conservation measures being undertaken.
Conservation measures that are not currently being implemented, but whose implementation would improve the structure and functions of Calaminarian grassland sites include: CL01 Management of habitats (others than agriculture and forest) to slow, stop or reverse natural processes; CC07 Habitat restoration/creation from resources, exploitation areas or areas damaged due to installation of renewable energy infrastructure; CA04 Reinstate appropriate agricultural practices to address abandonment, including mowing, grazing, burning or equivalent measures; CF04 Reduce/eliminate point source pollution to surface or ground waters from industrial, commercial, residential and recreational areas and activities.
The measures listed below are mainly preventative, rather than restorative:
Several sites are within National Parks and are protected from development (Glendasan [1.27 ha], Foxrock Mine [0.27 ha], Brockagh [0.11 ha], E. of L. Nahanagan [0.03 ha] – all in Wicklow Mountains National Park; Muckross Lake [0.027 ha] and Ross Island [0.055 ha] – both in Killarney National Park) or are in state ownership (e.g. Nr Connary Hall) and fenced off from disturbance.
Allihies Mountain Mine, the largest and perhaps most important Calaminarian grassland site in Ireland [1.32 ha], together with smaller sites nearby (N. of Caminches [0.026 ha], NE of Caminches [0.007 ha] and Dooneen [0.226 ha]) are preserved and presented by the Allihies Copper Mine Museum.  
A total of 12 of the 29 sites surveyed in 2008 and 2018 contain one or more bryophyte species that are legally protected under the Flora (Protection) Order, 2015 and thus subject to planning controls (Cephaloziella massalongi, C. nicholsonii, Ditrichum cornubicum, D. lineare, D. plumbicola).

measures_info:
Conservation measures were selected using information provided by NPWS (A. Bleasdale, pers. comm.). As there were no data on conservation measures for 8120 habitat recorded by the NSUH within the current reporting period, data from the previous reporting period were also utilised to inform Section 8 Conservation measures for this reporting period.
The maintenance of extensive sheep grazing (CA03) as a conservation measure was recorded twice by the NSUH in the previous reporting period (Perrin et al. (2013a, 2013c) as A04.02.02 (non-intensive sheep grazing) with a neutral effect. In some cases the maintenance of extensive sheep grazing is undertaken through the Department of Agriculture’s Green, Low Carbon, Agri-Environment Scheme (GLAS). This, however, is likely to be incidental for the 8120 habitat, based on it being present within an area managed under GLAS, rather than there being a targeted measure specific to this habitat. Details of participation in the GLAS scheme and of the uptake across various measures are not currently available. It is therefore not possible to precisely assess the effects of implementation of these measures.
It should be recognised that the management regimes of most areas of 8120 habitat are driven by the landowner rather than by any formal management plan or policy; therefore the continued operation of the management regimes currently in place is not guaranteed across the entire habitat.
Conservation measures that are not currently being widely implemented, but whose implementation would improve the Structure and functions of the habitat, are the control or removal of non-native species (CI03), particularly Epilobium brunnescens, and to adapt grazing activities (CA05) to try to alleviate overgrazing. It is not known what the best strategy for removal of Epilobium brunnescens would be, however, and it is speculated that removal would be expensive, difficult and time-consuming, with recurrent management necessary.

The implementation of the following conservation measures would improve the Structure and functions of this habitat:
CA13 Reduce/eliminate marine pollution from agricultural activities
CF04 Reduce/eliminate point source pollution to surface or ground waters from industrial, commercial, residential and recreational areas and activities.
Over-enrichment of Large shallow inlets and bays appears to be the most significant pressure acting on the habitat. Those Large shallow inlets and bays, and parts thereof, with low mixing capacity were recorded as being particularly vulnerable to the effects of nutrient enrichment.
Benthic dredging has had significant impacts on sensitive subtidal communities (Serpula vermicularis and maërl).
The establishment of the invasive alien species Sargassum muticum in eelgrass beds and maërl beds was a noted impact with the potential to lead to significant ecosystem change in the future.

measures_info: There is protection zone for rivers where clearcuts are not allowed and activities regarding protection (restriction of cutting) are implemented in Natura 2000 areas where specific zoning exist.

The effective management of all the sites under the Natura 2000 management plans has ensured the maintenance of the conservation status of the species in the period under review. The quality of the habitat of the species is good in the larger two sites (L-G?adira and is-Simar), but is not good in the other two smaller sites (Is-Salini and il-Mag?luq ta’ Marsaskala). Measures taken at il-Mag?luq ta’ Marsaskala where the condition of the coastal lagoon is reported as not good are actually showing an improvement in the habitat. These include the control of public access through the replacement of the fence surrounding the site, the installation of two new gates, the installation of CCTV cameras, and a patrolling schedule to reduce illegalities. Signs prohibiting dumping and littering were also installed on site, and a rat control program was initiated in 2018 to decrease the rat population attracted to the accumulated dumping and litter on site. Efforts were also made to decrease the population of feral ducks on site and invasive species such as Arundo donax, to make way for other species such as Juncus maritimus which provide the right ambient conditions for the reproduction and feeding of Aphanius fasciatus. On the other hand, although no improvement was observed yet at is-Salini, an agreement was reached between the  Government of Malta and BirdLife Malta to manage the site. In view of this, improvements in the habitat at this location are expected during the next reporting cycle.

Various measures have been carried out within the Natura 2000 site, MT0000018 - L-In?awi tal-Buskett u tal-Girgenti, through the 'Life Saving Buskett' project. The project aimed to conserve the various Annex I habitats composed of mature trees at Buskett, along the watercourse and the banks of Wied il-Luq (Poplar Valley). Measures taken included the stabilisation of soil in areas supporting these habitats and included the repair, restoration and rebuilding of retaining dry stone walls and arches defining part of the watercourse. Additionally these woodland habitats were restored through the planting of characteristic trees and, where applicable, the removal of invasive alien species.

Asp is broadly stocked by angling unions. Moreover, stocking juveniles reared from limited number of individuals can lead to genetic mixing, homogenization and destruction of intraspecific genetic diversity. Other measures should be takken as well (CJ02; CJ03;CH03;).

El proyecto LIFE+ RESECOM “Red de seguimiento para especies de flora y hábitats de interés comunitario en Aragón” [LIFE12 NAT/ES/000180], llevado a cabo durante el periodo 2013-2018, ha establecido una red de seguimiento de las plantas y hábitats de la Comunidad Autónoma de Aragón, con el objetivo de recopilar y generar información útil para el público y las administraciones, que permita obtener conocimiento a largo plazo sobre la dinámica de las poblaciones, especies y hábitats. En Galicia se está realizando el proyecto Migra-Minho, llevando a cabo acciones dirigidas a mejorar la calidad del hábitat y la conectividad del río Miño para especies migradoras (http://migraminho.org/). Estas medidas han sido realizadas en zonas protegidas como ZEC Baixo Miño [ES1140007] y ZEC Río Tea [ES1140006] (http://migraminho.org/downloads/ms_7679.pdf).
En Cantabria, se han realizado acciones en el río Miera encaminadas a mejorar la conectividad fluvial y a la adecuación de pasos y escalas para salmones en obstáculos transversales, bajo el proyecto LIFE Conservación de la Biodiversidad en el Río Miera [LIFE 13 NAT/ES/000899] (http://fnyh.org/life-nature-conservacion-de-la-biodiversidad-en-el-rio-miera).

Se ha realizado un plan de erradicación de Podarcis pityusensis, introducida en tiempos recientes en el peñón de San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, dentro del espacio ZEC San Juan de Gaztelugatxe [ES2130005]. De igual manera se deberían poner en marcha campañas de erradicación de gatos asilvestrados en zonas urbanas y periurbanas, al ejercer una importante presión depredatoria sobre las poblaciones de reptiles en general (Galán, 2013; Alarcos & Flechoso, 2012).

También se propone potenciar la presencia de refugios y lugares de nidificación dejando determinados resquicios durante las obras de rehabilitación de puentes o edificaciones (Ibáñez, 2007).

El proyecto LIFE LUTREOLA SPAIN “Nuevos enfoques en la conservación del visón europeo en España” [LIFE13 NAT/ES/001171], se ha desarrollado entre 2014-2018 en el País Vasco, La Rioja, Aragón y la Comunidad Valenciana (http://lifelutreolaspain.com).
Dentro de las acciones del proyecto Life Lutreola Spain se eliminó una población del visón americano recién establecida en 400 km fluviales en la cuenca del Ebro en La Rioja y en Álava. Durante el periodo de otoño de 2014 hasta finales de 2016 se han producido un total de 271 capturas,  posteriormente sólo se han capturado 3 ejemplares en 2017 y 2 más en 2018 en las vías de migración hacia el ámbito del proyecto (Comunicación personal: Informe final del proyecto LIFE, en redacción).

En La Rioja selleva a cabo el Plan de Recuperación del visón europeo (Mustela lutreola) en La Rioja (Decreto 55/2014) desde 2014, y ha logrado eliminar la presencia de visón americano mediante el uso de plataformas flotantes que ha permitido mejorar la eficacia en cuanto a la detección y captura de visón americano. En el periodo 2013-17 se han realizado 207 capturas. De ellos: 202 Trampeados, 3 Atropellados, 1 Muertos por otra causa
(https://www.iderioja.larioja.org/vct/index.php?c=386a6f784b555770494e307a4f7062685579366f56513d3d). Con este trabajo se ha logrado extraer un elevado número de ejemplares de la especie exótica de los ríos riojanos y se ha diseñado un protocolo de seguimiento de visón americano que tiene como objetivo evitar la recolonización de la especie invasora dentro del rango del visón europeo (detección temprana). 
Asimismo, en 2018 se han realizado las primeras liberaciones (refuerzo poblacional) en ríos riojanos de ejemplares criados en cautividad.
Entre 2010 y 2015 se llevó a cabo el proyecto LIFE Territorio Visón - Recuperación ambiental del Territorio Fluvial; espacio vital del visón europeo (Mustela lutreola) [LIFE09 NAT/ES/000531].
El Gobierno de Navarra  realiza campañas de detección y eliminación del visón americano con medios propios en toda la cuenca mediterránea.

Un réseau des stations de suivi a été mise en place dans les Pyrénées, avec une dizaine de placettes dans les départements de Hautes-Pyrénées, Haute-Garonne et Pyrénées-Orientales avec une méthodologie uniforme. Le réseau est hètèrogène par rapport aux acteurs, avec le CBNPMP comme nexe d'union,  et financé que partiellement, et pour tant assez faible à long terme. Les sites impliqués sont les suivants: site FR300921 Gabizos (65), FR300934 Rioumajou et Moudang (65); FR300923 Ossoue, Aspé, Cestrède (65); FR73120009 «Vallées du Lys, de la Pique et d’Oô en collaboration avec ONF (31) et la Réserve Naturel de Mantet (66).
Dans les placettes chaque pièce de bois a été identifiée avec un numéro sur un clou métallique dessinée sur un croquis pour faciliter le suivi, et les différents critères ont été notés sur une fiche. Les données ainsi obtenues vont permettre de comparer l’évolution de Buxbaumia viridis au sein de chaque pièce de bois, mais aussi sa dynamique entre les différentes pièces de bois, en établissant s’il y a eu des nouvelles colonisations, et attestant sa permanence sur la durée. Ces données vont également permettre d’observer l’évolution temporelle des différents stades de décomposition du bois et d’établir la durée de chaque stade. (Infante Sánchez 2015).

A signaler que des demandes de dérogation relatives à la destruction d’espèces végétales protégées qui affectent Buxbaumia viridis sont dèjà mises en marche d'une façon ponctuelle, par exemple en Ariège ou dans les Hautes-Pyrénées. Leur résultats sont donc à suivre.

Conservation measures are included in water management planning documents. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in water management activities and the system for evaluation of effectiveness of those measures is lacking.

Conservation objectives and conservation measures for Natura 2000 target species are included in Forestry management plans together with activities for reaching conservation objectives and thus Forestry management plans are considered Natura2000 management plans. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no data to what extent are these measures really contributing to the species conservation status.

Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures. Although there is no measure focused directly on the species, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features, e.g.  Preservation of high nature value grassland, which will probably have positive affect on the concerned species. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. Additionally, there is no systematic monitoring of the species, so there is no adequate data whether these measures are actually contributing to the species conservation status and to what extent are these measures really incorporated in agricultural activities.

Se han llevado a cabo escasas medidas para la conservación y recuperación de la especie en los últimos años. Las acciones programadas por las administraciones públicas han sido dirigidas a evitar los daños provocados por el lobo a la ganadería en su mayor parte.
En casi todas las Comunidades Autónomas donde existe presencia constante de la especie se han llevado a cabo seguimientos de su área de distribución y censos de los efectivos poblacionales. También se ha fomentado el uso de muladares y abandono de ganado muerto para aumentar la disponibilidad de alimento en algunas áreas ocupadas.
Destaca el 'Programa de Conservación del Lobo' en Andalucía (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/site/portalweb/menuitem.7e1cf46ddf59bb227a9ebe205510e1ca/?vgnextoid=216c2b7b3b42a110VgnVCM1000000624e50aRCRD&vgnextchannel=a1bff703e8207310VgnVCM1000001325e50aRCRD&lr=lang_es&vgnsecondoid=9220a2aecf09c410VgnVCM1000001325e50a____&param1=4), o el proyecto LIFE Southern Wolves: changing attitudes [LIFE15 GIE/ES/000962], ambos dirigidos a mejorar la percepción social de la especie en Sierra Morena y a fomentar la coexistencia entre el lobo y las actividades económicas rurales en Andalucía.

El proyecto LIFE+ RESECOM “Red de seguimiento para especies de flora y hábitats de interés comunitario en Aragón” [LIFE12 NAT/ES/000180], llevado a cabo durante el periodo 2013-2018, ha establecido una red de seguimiento de las plantas y hábitats de la Comunidad Autónoma de Aragón, con el objetivo de recopilar y generar información útil para el público y las administraciones, que permita obtener conocimiento a largo plazo sobre la dinámica de las poblaciones, especies y hábitats. Esta especie ha figurado en el listado de especies objetivo del proyecto (http://www.liferesecom.ipe.csic.es/index2.php?lang=es).

Las principales medidas de conservación idenfitifacas para mantener la actual amplitud geográfica, población y/o hábitat de la especie dentro de la Red Natura 2000 son:
- Planificación y seguimiento: mediante sistema de seguimiento
-Legislación, vigilancia y concienciación: mediante catálogos de protección legales
-Protección y gestión del hábitat: mediante medidas relacionadas con la ordenación territorial como la protección legal de hábitats y especies.
-Gestión de la especie objetivo: mediante reforzamiento y conservación exsitu en banco de germoplasma.

La especie está incluida en el 'Programa de Recuperación de Flora de las altas cumbres de Andalucía', bajo el cual se han llevado a cabo acciones de seguimiento de las poblaciones de la especie, además de la mejora del hábitat mediante la creación de varios cercados perimetrales para la exclusión de hervívoros, y medidas de refuerzo poblacional.
Como medidas de conservación ex situ esta especie se encuentra conservada en el Banco de Germoplasma Vegetal Andaluz (BGVA), de la Red Andaluza de Jardines Botánicos y Micológico. Está conservada en la colección del Jardín Botánico Torre del Vinagre, en Jaén (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/altas_cumbres/Arenaria%20nevadensis_2015.pdf).

Las medidas llevadas a cabo se han enumerado y proyectado bajo el 'Programa de Actuación del Plan de Recuperación y Conservación de Especies de Dunas, Arenales y Acantilados Costeros' durante el período 2015-2019 y actualmente en proceso de aplicación (http://www.juntadeandalucia.es/medioambiente/portal_web/web/temas_ambientales/biodiversidad/0_conservacion_biodiversidad/planes_conservacion_recuperacion/Programas%20de%20actuacion/programa_actuacion_dunas_y_arenales_%20anexo_VII.pdf).
Como medidas de conservación ex situ, esta especie se encuentra conservada en uno de los Jardines Botánicos pertenecientes a la RED Andaluza de Jardines Botánicos y Micológico. Así mismo existen semillas conservadas en el Banco de Germoplama Vegetal Andaluz (BGVA).

Measures for conservation of the species are included in watermanagement planing documents both inside and outside Natura2000 network. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in watermanagement activities. Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures. Although there is no measure focused directly on the species, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features, e.g.  Preservation of high nature value grassland, which will probably have positive affect on the concerned species. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. Additionally, there is no systematic monitoring of the species, so there is no data whether these measures are actually contributing to the species conservation status and to what extent are these measures really incorporated in agricultural activities.

Removal or remediation of physical barriers (CC04) to aid passage during spawning migration is an important consideration to promote the conservation status of twaite shad in Ireland (King & Linnane, 2004; King & Roche, 2008), especially in SACs where the availability of main stem channel from the estuary to the interior often falls well below the 75% criterion identified for this species (a target conservation objective for certain SAC rivers). This is particularly relevant for the River Barrow, a navigation river, where twaite shad are unable to ascend beyond the upper tidal limit due to a major weir. Detailed assessment of fish passability at all major barriers on the Irish SAC channels is underway and will be completed in 2019. To date these assessments have identified significant issues with many of these structures in regard to their capacity to permit upstream passage of adult diadromous species – Atlantic salmon and sea lamprey (Barry et al. 2018) – and it is likely that the same conclusions would apply to twaite shad. 
Management of hunting (CG02) is pertinent to twaite shad conservation. Hunting, in the form of angling for leisure purposes, is undertaken for twaite shad to a limited degree during May, annually, during the adult spawning run. Currently, no measures have been introduced at state level to manage the angling effort. While the Irish Specimen Fish Committee, which monitors catches of specimen-sized twaite shad, urges a fish-friendly catch-and-release policy, it is likely that mortalities do occur from angling during this vulnerable phase in their life-cycle.
Conservation measure CG05 (reduce bycatch and incidental killing of non-target species) is relevant to the twaite shad as the species may be vulnerable to capture at sea as bycatch. It is not apparent that any measure can be undertaken to prevent such bycatch. Staff of the various state fisheries agencies (Marine Institute (MI), Sea Fisheries Protection Authority (SFPA) and Bord Iascaigh Mhara (BIM)) liaise with Inland Fisheries Ireland and retain material of twaite shad that presents as bycatch in commercial ports. A very small amount of material becomes available and may indicate a low level of bycatch.  
Any measure to reduce diffuse pollution to surface or ground waters from agricultural activities (CA11) would benefit water quality in rivers. This would have a knock-on beneficial effect on twaite shad during the freshwater spawning phase, when spawning grounds can experience substantial filamentous algal growth. 
Conservation measure CI03 (management, control or eradication of other invasive alien species) is relevant in regard to the spawning areas of twaite shad, at least in regard to the River Barrow. Large accumulations of the Asian clam Corbicula fluminea have been recorded within kilometres of the twaite shad spawning ground. There is potential for this invasive species to spread and ultimately cover the river bed in the spawning ground of the shad. Fertilised shad eggs dropping to the river bed may be enveloped by the clam populations and substantial mortalities may occur. Although not part of a targeted conservation measure for twaite shad, a dredging operation on the river bed within the current 6-year monitoring period yielded a very large quantity of C. fluminea. This was disposed of to landfill. Such measures may continue to be necessary as a conservation strategy to manage the C. fluminea population present. 
Management of professional/commercial fishing (CG01) would be expected to help alleviate pressures on marine prey species of twaite shad.

Having identified the problems that sturgeon face in the Lower Danube, the INCDPM-Bucharest proposes a series of measures that may improve the future conservation state of the sturgeon species:
1.	Involving commercial fisherman in research programs that have the aim to make an inventory of monitor fish species from the waterways.
2.	Awareness-rising campaigns regarding the status of fish species, their importance for human consumption and sustainable ways to use this biological resource.
3.	Fitting the commercial boats with GPS tracking devices, in order to monitor their activities and the location of these activities. This would attenuate the need for extra personnel and would ensure better control.  
4.	A reduction in the number of authorizations issued for commercial fishing combined with a re-training scheme for fisherman.
5.	Banning of fishing tools that have a high probability of catching sturgeon species (whose fishing is prohibited till 2021).
6.	Implementing restocking program with juvenile sturgeon so that the wild populations of sturgeon are sustained and wild stocks are recharged faster.
7.	Banning of commercial fishing during the migration time of sturgeon species that accidental capture is minimalized.
8.	Establishing transboundary joint aquatic resources management programs that offer sustainable and fair management for each of the coastal States.
9.	Improving legislation on scientific activities which have the main goal of fish monitoring, especially monitoring of protected species (i.e. sturgeons and fish species of community interest). These measures could ensure realistic predictions on the conservation state, evaluate the effects/efficiency of measures like banning of commercial fishing of sturgeon species and restocking programs.
10.	 Realization of cooperation protocols between researchers involved in fish monitoring and authorities that can control and enforce the law (Police, Border Police and Naval Police) and the authority that manages the aquatic resources (National Agency of Fisheries and Aquaculture) in order to minimize the effects of poaching.

El proyecto LIFE LimnoPirineus - Restauración de hábitats leníticos y especies acuáticas de interés comunitario de alta montaña en los Pirineos [LIFE13 NAT/ES/001210] restaurará el funcionamiento ecológico de 19 lagos de alta montaña a través de la erradicación – o la reducción significativa - de especies de peces exóticas invasoras. Establecerá nuevas poblaciones de especies de anfibios objetivo - Rana temporaria, Alytes obstetricans y Euproctus asper - en los lagos en los que los peces alóctonos hayan sido retirados. La principal medida de conservación para la especie a largo plazo tanto dentro como fuera de la Red Natura 2000 consiste en la protección legal de hábitat y especies.  
La especie ha sido objeto de dos proyectos LIFE en las últimas décadas: LIFE FLORA -"Conservación de áreas con flora amenazada en la isla de Menorca" (2001-2004) [LIFE 2000/NAT/E/7355]; y LIFE+ RENEIX "Restauración de hábitats de especies prioritarias en la isla de Menorca" (2009-2014) [LIFE+ 07 NAT/E/000756].
Bajo la ejecución de estos proyectos se han llevado a cabo medidas de conservación y restauración del hábitat, como actuaciones de erradicación de plantas exóticas invasoras, eliminación de viales y accesos incontrolados, plantaciones de especies autóctonas, etc. (http://lifereneix.cime.es).

Se recomienda invertir esfuerzos en recolectar mayor información sobre la biología y ecología de la especie y llevar a cabo medidas de conservación ex situ, como la reintroducción de la especie a partir de semillas de las poblaciones portuguesas.

Measures for conservation of Lyceana dispar are included in watermanagement planing documents both inside and outside Natura2000 network. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no adequate data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in watermanagement activities. Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures.
Although there is no measure focused directly on Lycaena dispar, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features, e.g.  Preservation of high nature value grassland, which will probably have positive affect on the concerned species. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. Additionally, there is no systematic monitoring of the species, so there is no adequate data whether these measures are actually contributing to the species conservation status and to what extent are these measures really incorporated in agricultural activities.

Se ha llevado a cabo una gestión específica de la especie objetivo mediante: conservación ex situ basada en semillas guardadas en el 'Jardí Botànic de Sóller' en Mallorca y cultivo; conservación in situ consistente en un programa de rehabilitación por la Universidad de Baleares desde 2003 además de un programa de erradicación de especies introducidas e invasivas de uña de gato (Carpobrotus edulis) en las Islas Baleares.

The maintenance of extensive cattle and sheep grazing (recorded under code CA03) is the most frequent conservation measure applied to the habitat. In some cases, the maintenance of extensive cattle and sheep grazing is undertaken through the Department of Agriculture’s Green, Low-Carbon, Agri-Environment Scheme (GLAS).  Details of participation in the GLAS scheme and of the uptake across various measures are not currently available. It is therefore not possible to precisely assess the effects of implementation of these measures in the SAC network.
It should be recognised that the management regimes of most habitats in P. ralfsii localities are driven by the landowner(s) rather than by any formal management plan or policy; therefore, the continued operation of the management regimes currently in place is not guaranteed, for e.g. commonage grazing at a number of localities.
Measure CF01 (Manage conversion of land for construction and development of infrastructures) refers to the correct implementation of planning policies, for example, carrying out appropriate assessments of Annex I habitats in SACs prior to undertaking construction projects for residential, commercial, industrial and recreational purposes, such as golf courses and club houses. All 30 P. ralfsii populations are listed as Qualifying Interests (QIs) for the 21 SACs within which they occur and therefore are protected by the Birds and Natural Habitats Regulations (Statutory Instrument No. 477/2011) which regulates any plans or projects that may negatively impact on the habitat for the species. There is also a list of Activities Requiring Consent (ARCs) that are only granted if they do not negatively impact on the QIs within an SAC. Any damaging activity that impacts the conservation status of P. ralfsii populations is regulated under the Environment Liability Regulations 2008 (S.I. 547 of 2008). While P. ralfsii is classified as Least Concern in the Republic of Ireland (Lockhart et al., 2012b), Ireland’s international responsibility to conserve the species is recognised and P. ralfsii is protected under the Flora (Protection) Order, 2015 (Statutory Instrument No. 356 of 2015); as such, it is illegal to collect the species without a licence or damage/destroy the species or its habitat. Legislation, however, does not in itself constitute a management plan; rather it is a tool by which plans may be enacted. Failure to enforce legislation is as damaging as not having a plan in place or not implementing it. Therefore, it is important that any laws or regulations that safeguard P. ralfsii be implemented and enforced.
At the three localities where P. ralfsii was not recorded during the 2016-2018 survey (Pr17a SW of Lough Naparka, Pr17c Kilshannig and Pr19 West of Inny Ferry), conservation measure CA05 Adapt mowing, grazing and other equivalent agricultural activities should be implemented to achieve a level of appropriate grazing that makes the habitat for the species more favourable for P. ralfsii.

Esto se debe a las medidas de prevención: los rebaños se han agrupado para en grupos mayores (> 1000 cabezas) y se contrataron pastores para gestionarlas. Las condiciones de vida de los pastores en las montañas se mejoraron a través de la construcción y remodelación de cabañas y refugios. Los Equipos de Apoyo y Conservación del Oso realizan el seguimiento de la población de osos pardos y proporcionaron información a los ganaderos, pastores, cazadores, turistas y ayuntamientos.

L'évaluation des impacts des pratiques de gestion des espaces naturels en site Natura 2000 est inexistante sur l'espèce. Les mesure de gestion sont mises en oeuvre de manière totalement empirique, sans évaluation.

Des propositions ont été faites en COPIL Natura 2000 pour le contrôle de la fréquentation des berges et la mise en réserve de noyaux de populations les plus importants. Ces propositions restent en attente de mise en application. Une politique a été mise en place pour le contrôle des rejets d'eaux usées dans les étangs et les bassins versants. Ponctuellement, des actions locales sont menées (Campagne de sensibilisation auprès des personnes pratiquant des activités nautiques, 
Pose de clôture par une association pour éviter le piétinement par les animaux d'élevage).

La mise en place d'un réseau de stations réservoirs a été engagée ces dernières années, via la mise en place d'un plan de gestion (en cours en 2018) visant à préserver durablement les populations de 17 stations réservoirs, correspondant à des populations clés en Loire jalonnant l'aire de répartition de l'espèce. La sélection des stations repose en particulier sur les résultats d'une étude génétique réalisée sur la méta-population ligérienne (Metais et al., 2009). Celle-ci a notamment permis de déterminer la distance suffisante entre deux stations réservoirs de façon à garantir un brassage génétique suffisant.

En Champagne-Ardenne, la revalorisation des états de conservation globale de l'espèce entre 2009/11 et 2016/17, est moins flagrante pour les secteurs de région naturelle que dans les sites Natura 2000 (CPIE Pays de Soulaines, 2018).
Pour protéger l’espèce, il faut conserver des populations de la plante-hôte (Peucédans) et éviter absolument le fauchage intégral en fin de saison (Lépi'Net).

Barbel is broadly stocked by angling unions. This measure helped to stabilize barbel populations, however the most important measures leading to the habitat and river connectivity restoration have not been takken. Stocking alone cann't ensure the long term survival of local populations and costitution of self-sustainable populations. Moreover, stocking juveniles reared from limited number of individuals can lead to genetic mixing, homogenization and destruction of intraspecific genetic diversity. Other measures should be takken as well (CJ02; CJ03;CH03;).

Las principales medidas de conservación identificadas para mantener la actual amplitud geográfica, población, y/o hábitat para la especie, tanto dentro como fuera de la Red Natura 2000 son: 
-Gestión de la especie objetivo mediante la conservación exsitu en banco de germoplasma.
-Legislación, vigilancia y conservación mediante catálogos de protección legales.
-Planificación y seguimiento de la especie mediante un plan de gestión y un sistema de seguimiento.

Se deberían tomar medidas de restauración fluvial. Se han identificado medidas de conservación de los hábitats como la protección de los bosques de caducifolios autóctonos, y la promoción de técnicas y aprovechamientos forestales que preserven los árboles que ofrecen mayores oportunidades de refugio para esta especie.

El mantenimiento y mejora de la calidad del hábitat es la medida más efectiva para la especie.
Además, se considera necesario evitar que los gatos domésticos o asilvestrados tengan acceso a zonas donde pueda haber gatos monteses, ya que, además de la hibridación, desplazan a los propios gatos monteses (Sarmento et al., 2009). Esta medida es actualmente muy complicada en España, donde se permite mantener colonias felinas en el medio ambiente natural y el control sobre los movimientos de los gatos domésticos es muy difícil de hacer.
También es necesaria una regulación más restrictiva del control de depredadores para evitar la captura de gatos monteses en las trampas e intensififcar los controles sobre los cebos envenenados.

However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no adequate data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in watermanagement activities. Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures. Although there is no measure focused directly on the species, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. As the pressures impacting on the Slender Naiad, Najas flexilis, have been documented, the conservation measures required to maintain or restore it are broadly understood. These include:
1. reversal of drainage impacts/restoration of hydrological regime, and
2. reducing pollution (with dissolved and/or particulate nutrients, humic substances, organic matter and fine sediment/turbidity) from agricultural, forestry, turf-cutting, golf course and domestic and urban waste-water sources.
Significant progress has been made with domestic waste-water treatment systems through the inspection regime (under the Domestic Waste-Water Treatment Regulations) and associated grant scheme. There is also on-going substantial investment by Irish Water in urban waste-water treatment and collection systems. Option (a) “Measures identified, but none yet taken” was chosen, however, as the majority of the most important measures identified, particularly in relation to agriculture and forestry, have not yet been implemented. 
No targeted conservation measures are currently being undertaken to restore or enhance Najas flexilis populations within SACs. As option (a) was used, fields 9.2 to 9.5 were not filled.
The Programmes of Measures under the WFD River Basin Management Plan will help improve water quality generally, however the WFD’s default option of “Good ecological status” may not be sufficient to return Najas flexilis to Favourable conservation status. While further work is needed to identify effective, practical and iterative solutions and to establish interim targets, this species is associated with high water quality, i.e. the absence of eutrophication impacts: naturally low dissolved nutrients, clear water and low algal growth; and is considered to require oligotrophic or WFD high status in Ireland (NPWS, 2013; O Connor, 2013, 2015).
Changes in the state of aquatic ecosystems, such as nutrient enrichment or hydrological changes, have multiple drivers and sources, especially when, as for Article 17, summarised at regional or national scale. Therefore, a large number of the new standard list of conservation measures would be applicable and some measures may have more than one function (e.g. changes to grazing regime to prevent pollution, restore hydrological function, enhance biodiversity, etc.).
Based on the assessment of the pressures impacting on Najas flexilis, measures are required to address drainage impacts (CA15, CC07, CB14), pollution from agriculture (CA09, CA10, CA11, CA15, CA12, CA01, CA03, CA05, CA06, CA08), peat extraction (CC08, CC09, CC01, CC02), forestry (CB10, CB14, CB09, CB11, CB04, CB01), on-site systems (unsewered) (CF05) and urban waste-water (CF04). Measures may also be necessary to address abstraction pressures (CF11), and other hydromorphological pressures from a variety of sources, including damage to wetland hydrology (examples of appropriate measures include CA08, CE06, CF10, CF02, CF03, CJ03, etc.).
The measures listed were implemented by some, but not all federal states with a share of the distribution area.

Conservation measures CS01 and CB06 were partially implemented by the federal states.  CB06 was implemented only inside Natura 2000. 

The other measures mentioned are necessary but have not yet been implemented.

To achieve an improvement in conservation status, a transboundary approach concerning conservation measures is obligatory.
Successful conservation measures may furthermore encompass measures related to livestock guarding, damage compensation, prevention of and/or proper reaction to problematic behaviour of single individuals and educational work.
Of the measures mentioned above, compensatory payments have already been implemented, and  there are recommendations in the management plan for dealing with problematic single individuals.
Some conservation measures to enhance the habitat of the species have been implemented in Lower Austria, Upper Austria and Salzburg.

Conservation measure CA11 was partially implemented by some federal states. 
The other measures mentioned are necessary but have not yet been implemented.

Se ha señalado la importancia del mantenimiento de un gran número de árboles viejos en los bosques, y se ha enfatizado especialmente en el importante rol que cumplen ciertas especies secundarias de arbustos forestales, tales como el acebo (Ilex aquifolium), las cuales pueden minimizar los efectos de talar los árboles más grandes. Considerando que las colonias de Myotis alcathoe han sido solamente encontradas en bosques maduros (con 50-100 árboles muertos por hectárea), las directrices del actual manejo forestal (que recomiendan una mínima densidad de 5-10 árboles muertos por hectárea), podrían ser insuficientes para proporcionar un hábitat adecuado para esta especie de murciélago (Coronado et al., 2017).

Las principales medidas de conservación para la especie son:
-Planificación y seguimiento: Plan de gestión.
-Legislación, vigilancia y concienciación: Catálogos de protección legales.
-Gestión de la especie objetivo: Conservación ex situ mediante recolección de propágulos y propagación artificial.

L'évaluation des impacts des pratiques de gestion des espaces naturels en site Natura 2000 est inexistante sur l'espèce. Les mesure de gestion sont mises en œuvre de manière totalement empirique, sans évaluations.

Si des mesures de restauration d'habitat sont mises en place en priorité (continuité écologique, gestion des berges, qualité de l'eau), les mesures pour diminuer la mortalité sont aussi nombreuses (collisions routières, mortalité accidentelle par piégeage)
Pour protéger l’espèce, il faut conserver des populations de la plante-hôte (Peucédans) et éviter absolument le fauchage intégral en fin de saison (Lépi'Net).

La mise en oeuvre d'un Plan National d'Action relative au Barbeau méridional est en cours d'instruction. Ce PNA permettre de fédérer les di?érents acteurs autour de la préservation de l'espèce au regard de son caractère emblématique. Il pourrait contribuer à a?ermir la protection des biotopes et à conduire des ré?exions pour adopter des mesures de gestion quantitative à l’échelle des bassins versants (càd au delà des périmètres de protection de biotope ou des SIC N2000).

Deux actions majeures mises en place :
- pour la restauration du fonctionnement hydrologique : programme Life Tourbière du Jura 2014-2020 (http://www.life-tourbieres-jura.fr), complété par un diagnostic précis du site de Bannans via le programme Natura 2000 (2017-2019).
- programme de renforcement et de réintroduction de populations de saxifrage oeil-de-bouc dans le Jura français initié en 2017 par le Conservatoire botanique national de Franche-Comté (2017-2023).
Des mesures seraient à mettre en oeuvre au moins sur les populations de Ligulaire à faibles effectifs.

Measures for conservation of Lyceana dispar are included in watermanagement planing documents both inside and outside Natura2000 network. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no adequate data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in watermanagement activities. Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures. Although there is no measure focused directly on Lycaena dispar, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features, e.g.  Preservation of high nature value grassland, which will probably have positive affect on the concerned species. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. Additionally, there is no systematic monitoring of the species, so there is no adequate data whether these measures are actually contributing to the species conservation status and to what extent are these measures really incorporated in agricultural activities

El mantenimiento y mejora de la calidad del hábitat es la medida más efectiva para la especie.
Además, se considera necesario evitar que los gatos domésticos o asilvestrados tengan acceso a zonas donde pueda haber gatos monteses, ya que, además de la hibridación, desplazan a los propios gatos monteses (Sarmento et al., 2009). Esta medida es actualmente muy complicada en España, donde se permite mantener colonias felinas en el medio ambiente natural y el control sobre los movimientos de los gatos domésticos es muy difícil de hacer.
También es necesaria una regulación más restrictiva del control de depredadores para evitar la captura de gatos monteses en las trampas e intensififcar los controles sobre los cebos envenenados.

De focus van de acties die voorgesteld worden in dit SBP ligt op de verdere verbetering van de waterkwaliteit, structuurherstel van waterlopen, een aangepast waterloopbeheer en het verbinden van geïsoleerde populaties door het oplossen van vismigratieknelpunten. Al deze acties zijn te beschouwen als verdere concretiseringen van acties die reeds voorzien zijn in de stroomgebiedbeheerplannen in uitvoering van de Europese kaderrichtlijn Water. Verder is er ook aandacht voor herintroductie (omwille van de beperkte dispersiecapaciteit van deze soorten) en voor het beheer van invasieve exoten die een bedreiging kunnen vormen voor deze vissoorten. Ten slotte is het noodzakelijk om de verspreiding van de soorten zowel op Vlaams niveau als gebiedsgericht afdoende te monitoren, zodat acties en maatregelen gericht kunnen worden ingezet en geëvalueerd.

The following conservation measure is recommended but is not yet being taken:
Conservation measure ‘CI03 Management, control or eradication of other invasive alien species’ is recommended to control the spread of invasive species in locations where V. speciosa sporophyte colonies occur. The presence of invasive non-native species at the six populations detailed in section 8.3 above is a cause for concern for the habitat where the colonies occur.
The presence of invasive non-native species is a difficult impacting activity to quantify or to manage for populations where V. speciosa occurs. The presence of such species provides shelter and canopy cover, and helps maintain sufficient relative humidity at locations where V. speciosa colonies occur. Therefore, the removal of such species in the immediate vicinity of V. speciosa colonies is not advised, as such a drastic measure may pose a greater threat to the V. speciosa colonies than leaving the invasive species in situ. However, the control of their spread in locations where V. speciosa colonies occur is a conservation measure that would be expected to have a positive outcome for the future of these habitats. Any control plan implemented must be undertaken sensitively and with the requirements of V. speciosa in mind. The possible future effects resulting from this threat are based on expert opinion and from the results of surveys carried out where these invasive species occur.

Se ha señalado la importancia del mantenimiento de un gran número de árboles viejos en los bosques, y se ha enfatizado especialmente en el importante rol que cumplen ciertas especies secundarias de arbustos forestales, tales como Ilex aquifolium, las cuales pueden minimizar los efectos de talar los árboles más grandes. Considerando que las colonias de Myotis alcanthoe han sido solamente encontradas en bosques maduros (con 50-100 árboles muertos por hectárea), las directrices del actual manejo forestal (que recomiendan una mínima densidad de 5-10 árboles muertos por hectárea), podrían ser insuficientes para proporcionar un hábitat adecuado para esta especie de murciélago (Coronado et al., 2017).
Algunas de las medidas propuestas están encaminadas a la restauración del hábitat (Galán, 2017).
Se han realizado colocaciones de cajas nido especiales y otros refugios para quirópteros en zonas de Navarra como bosques, construcciones históricas y parques y entornos urbanos.

El mantenimiento y mejora de la calidad del hábitat es la medida más efectiva para la especie.
Además, se considera necesario evitar que los gatos domésticos o asilvestrados tengan acceso a zonas donde pueda haber gatos monteses, ya que, además de la hibridación, desplazan a los propios gatos monteses (Sarmento et al., 2009). Esta medida es actualmente muy complicada en España, donde se permite mantener colonias felinas en el medio ambiente natural y el control sobre los movimientos de los gatos domésticos es muy difícil de hacer.
También es necesaria una regulación más restrictiva del control de depredadores para evitar la captura de gatos monteses en las trampas e intensififcar los controles sobre los cebos envenenados.

Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures. Although there is no measure focused directly on the species, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features, e.g.  Preservation of high nature value grassland, which will probably have positive affect on the concerned species. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. Additionally, there is no systematic monitoring of the species, so there is no adequate data whether these measures are actually contributing to the species conservation status and to what extent are these measures really incorporated in agricultural activities. Conservation objectives and conservation measures for Natura 2000 target species are included in Forestry management plans together with activities for reaching conservation objectives and are considered Natura2000 management plans. These measures could contribute to the conservation status of also this species. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no adequate data to what extent are these measures really contributing to the species conservation status.

Measures for conservation of the species are included in watermanagement planing documents both inside and outside Natura2000 network. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no adequate data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in watermagement activities. Thirteen Special Areas of Conservation (SAC) have been designated with grey seals as a qualifying feature (grade A-C), which are listed on the JNCC website: (UK0017072) Berwickshire and North Northumberland Coast (England inshore & Scotland inshore); (UK0012712) Cardigan Bay/ Bae Ceredigion (Wales inshore); (UK0017096) Faray and Holm of Faray (Scotland inshore); (UK0030170) Humber Estuary (England inshore); (UK0030172) Isle of May (Scotland inshore); (UK0013694) Isles of Scilly Complex (England inshore); (UK0013114) Lundy (England inshore); (UK0012694 Monach Islands (Scotland inshore); (UK0012696) North Rona (Scotland inshore); (UK0013116) Pembrokeshire Marine/ Sir Benfro Forol (Wales inshore); (UK0013117) Pen Llyn a`r Sarnau/ Lleyn Peninsula and the Sarnau (Wales inshore); (UK0030384) The Maidens (Northern Ireland inshore); (UK0030289) Treshnish Isles (Scotland inshore).Other sites (MPAs) designated under domestic legislation in the UK (e.g. Marine Conservation Zones (MCZ); Site of Special Scientific Interest (SSSI) Area of Special Scientific Interest (ASSI)) have grey seals as 'features' and contribute to the conservation and management of the species. Furthermore, under Section 117 of the Marine (Scotland) Act 2010, Scottish Ministers, consulting with the Natural Environment Research Council (NERC), are permitted to designate specific seal haul-out sites to provide additional protection for seals from intentional or reckless harassment. 194 seal haul-out sites, including key breeding sites along with a number of additional specific sites proposed by respondents, were designated through The Protection of Seals (Designation of Haul-Out Sites) (Scotland) Order 2014 with the addition of the River Ythan in 2017. https://www.gov.scot/Topics/marine/marine-environment/species/19887/20814/haulouts. The UK has been committed to supporting several international agreements and conventions on the conservation of marine mammals and the marine environment in general. For example: The Convention on Migratory Species (CMS); the Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the North-East Atlantic (OSPAR). The UK Government funds a national strandings scheme, ongoing since 1990, with a Scottish arm (Scottish Marine Animal Strandings Scheme) which monitors causes of death in seals.

A medium-intensity heterogeneous management (such as military training or rather extensive grazing) while preventing shrub and woodland encroachment seems to be the appropriate conservation management. Remarkably, the beetle is not primarily associated with short-sward steppe, but prefers comparatively humid tall-grass stretches within grassland areas and also enters agricultural set-aside areas. Any management thus needs to preserve tall-grass stretches within the steppe habitat.

To ensure adequate assessment of wind farm impacts on large carnivores Manual for assessment of wind farms impact on large carnivores was  developed. These manual can be used for all environmental and nature related assessment procedures (Strategic environmental assessment (SEA), environmental impact assessment (EIA), and Appropriate assessment (AA)). Five sensitivity maps have been developed: for each species of large carnivores individually (bear, wolf, lynx), for bear dens and for all three species together. The sensitivity map indicates importance of certain area for each species of large carnivores and defines nine classes of habitat sensitivity. Furthermore, green bridges infrastructure was build on main Dalmatian highway.

Besides several obligations set in the Common Agricultural Policy, within Rural Development Programme there are measures available which contribute to the conservation of biodiversity of agricultural and forestry ecosystems, especially Agri-environment-climate measures. Although there is no measure focused directly on the species, there are several measures aimed at maintaining existing extensive agricultural practices and agricultural landscape features, e.g.  Preservation of high nature value grassland, which will probably have positive affect on the concerned species. However, Agri-environment-climate measures are volontary and their uptake is currently not at high level. Additionally, there is no systematic monitoring of the species, so there is no adequate data whether these measures are actually contributing to the species conservation status and to what extent are these measures really incorporated in agricultural activities. Conservation objectives and conservation measures for Natura 2000 target species are included in Forestry management plans together with activities for reaching conservation objectives and are considered Natura2000 management plans. These measures could contribute to the conservation status of also this species. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no adequate data to what extent are these measures really contributing to the species conservation status.

Measures for conservation of the species are included in watermanagement planing documents both inside and outside Natura2000 network. However, as there is no systematic monitoring, there is no data to what extent are these measures really incorporated in watermanagement activities. De focus van de acties die voorgesteld worden in dit SBP ligt op de verdere verbetering van de waterkwaliteit, structuurherstel van waterlopen, een aangepast waterloopbeheer en het verbinden van geïsoleerde populaties door het oplossen van vismigratieknelpunten. Al deze acties zijn te beschouwen als verdere concretiseringen van acties die reeds voorzien zijn in de stroomgebiedbeheerplannen in uitvoering van de Europese kaderrichtlijn Water. Verder is er ook aandacht voor herintroductie (omwille van de beperkte dispersiecapaciteit van deze soorten) en voor het beheer van invasieve exoten die een bedreiging kunnen vormen voor deze vissoorten. Ten slotte is het noodzakelijk om de verspreiding van de soorten zowel op Vlaams niveau als gebiedsgericht afdoende te monitoren, zodat acties en maatregelen gericht kunnen worden ingezet en geëvalueerd.

El proyecto LIFE LimnoPirineus - Restauración de hábitats leníticos y especies acuáticas de interés comunitario de alta montaña en los Pirineos [LIFE13 NAT/ES/001210] restaurará el funcionamiento ecológico de 19 lagos de alta montaña a través de la erradicación – o la reducción significativa - de especies de peces exóticas invasoras. Establecerá nuevas poblaciones de especies de anfibios objetivo - Rana temporaria, Alytes obstetricans y Euproctus asper - en los lagos en los que los peces alóctonos hayan sido retirados. El proyecto LIFE+ RESECOM “Red de seguimiento para especies de flora y hábitats de interés comunitario en Aragón” [LIFE12 NAT/ES/000180], llevado a cabo durante el periodo 2013-2018, ha establecido una red de seguimiento de las plantas y hábitats de la Comunidad Autónoma de Aragón, con el objetivo de recopilar y generar información útil para el público y las administraciones, que permita obtener conocimiento a largo plazo sobre la dinámica de las poblaciones, especies y hábitats. Esta especie ha figurado en el listado de especies objetivo del proyecto (http://www.liferesecom.ipe.csic.es/index2.php?lang=es).

Las principales medidas de conservación propuestas para esta especie tanto dentro como fuera de la Red Natura 2000 son:
-Legislación, vigilancia y concienciación: Protección legal de hábitats y especies y establecimiento de áreas protegidas.
-Medidas relacionadas con la ordenación del territorio: Restauración/Mejora de hábitats forestales, otras medidas relacionadas con los humedales, restauración/mejora de la calidad del agua y del régimen hidrológico, gestión de las captaciones de agua y gestión de las capacitaciones de agua.
-Medidas relacionadas con la especie: Medidas de manejo específicas para una especie o un grupo de especies.

Las principales acciones han sido tomadas encaminadas a la restauración y la creación de charcas y masas de agua adecuadas (Ekos Estudios Ambientales S.L.U., 2017). La traslocación de adultos, juveniles y renacuajos a nuevos puntos de reproducción fueron realizados por el 'Plan de reintroducción y seguimiento' en el período 1998-2003 y en años sucesivos, para mejorar la conectividad y el acervo genético poblacional (Etxazarreta & Rubio, 2003).
Hay dos charcas en ZEC Ría del Oria [ES2120010] restauradas y bajo supervisión realizados por un plan de restauración y seguimiento (Ekos Estudios Ambientales S.L.U., 2017).
Es necesario tener en cuenta a la especie en las reformas o arreglos de edificios

Se están llevando a cabo diferentes planes de gestión de la especie para ser considerados a la hora de fijar cupos de caza. Dichos planes se están desarrollando en diferentes reservas de caza. Según Prada & Herrero (2013) es importante recalcar la importancia de las reservas de caza en la recuperación de especies cinegéticas en general, y de la cabra montés en la Cordillera Cantábrica en particular. Estos territorios cinegéticos de gestión pública, declarados en terrenos con excepcionales potencialidades cinegéticas, se crearon para fomentar la caza, controlar del furtivismo, fomentar el turismo cinegético y, sobre todo, recuperar las poblaciones de especies cinegéticas en un momento en el que muchas de ellas tenían diezmadas sus poblaciones.

Las siguientes medidas de conservación deberían ser incorporadas:
CA05 Adapt mowing, grazing and other equivalent agricultural activities
CB01 Prevent conversion of (semi-) natural habitats into forests and of (semi-)natural forests into intensive forest plantation
CC03 Adapt/manage renewable energy installation, facilities and operation.

Se ha señalado la importancia del mantenimiento de un gran número de árboles viejos en los bosques, y se ha enfatizado especialmente en el importante rol que cumplen ciertas especies secundarias de arbustos forestales, tales como Ilex aquifolium, las cuales pueden minimizar los efectos de talar los árboles más grandes. Considerando que las colonias de Myotis alcanthoe han sido solamente encontradas en bosques maduros (con 50-100 árboles muertos por hectárea), las directrices del actual manejo forestal (que recomiendan una mínima densidad de 5-10 árboles muertos por hectárea), podrían ser insuficientes para proporcionar un hábitat adecuado para esta especie de murciélago (Coronado et al., 2017).
Se han llevado a cabo estudios recientes sobre quirópteros foretales en ZEC Gasteizko mendi garaiak/Montes altos de Vitoria [ES2110015] y en ZEC Izki [ES2110019], y se han descubierto nuevas poblaciones en enclaves de bosque atlántico (https://www.vitoria-gasteiz.org/docs/wb021/contenidosEstaticos/adjuntos/es/06/87/70687.pdf) (https://www.araba.eus/cs/Satellite?blobcol=urldata&blobheader=application%2Fpdf&blobheadername1=Content-disposition&blobheadername2=pragma&blobheadervalue1=inline%3B+filename%3D&blobheadervalue2=public&blobkey=id&blobtable=MungoBlobs&blobwhere=1224094950425&ssbinary=true).

Mediante un Proyecto LIFE + Lampropeltis y acciones de gestión posteriores se han llevado a cabo campañas de control de la población de Lampropeltis getulae californiae, mediante sistemas de vigilancia y captura, mejora en el conocimientyo de la población y campañas de concienciación y participación social.

Se han realizado colocaciones de cajas-refugio para murciélagos en Navarra (http://www.mnconsultors.com/pubs_files/201512848602013_cajas_murcielagos_alcalde_campion_et_al.pdf).
Se recomienda reservar rodales o pequeños grupos de árboles con refugios abundantes alrededor del árbol o árboles ocupados por nóctulos (por ejemplo, en un radio de 50 a 100 m) en entornos urbanos. El emplazamiento de cajas refugio es una medida transitoria muy buena para asentar una colonia de nóctulos (Camprodron & Guixé, 2013).
Se conservan semillas de algunas poblaciones en el banco de germoplasma del Jardín Botánico de Blanes.
